question_text: Which of the following correctly describes what happens on the spring equinox (around 21 March) in the UK?

options.label: A
options.text: The day is the longest of the year because the Earth is tilted most directly towards the Sun.

options.label: B
options.text: Day and night are approximately equal in length (12 hours each).

options.label: C
options.text: The Southern Hemisphere also experiences spring and the same weather as the UK.

options.label: D
options.text: The Sun is at its closest point to Earth, causing warmer temperatures.

explanation: On an equinox, the Sun is directly above the equator, so all parts of Earth experience equal day and night. Option A confuses equinox with solstice. Option C ignores that opposite hemispheres have opposite seasons. Option D incorrectly links distance to seasons.
